text: The patient has several small incisions on the abdomen, through which trocars are introduced for robotic adhesiolysis.
Robotic instruments are inserted into the abdomen through the trocars after the robot is connected, preparing for the adhesiolysis procedure.
The surgeon uses robotic scissors, visible at the top of the image, which are bent to allow cutting at various angles without needing to move the instrument in and out of the patient's abdomen.
The surgeon continues the dissection using an instrument equipped with power control, applying cauterization or ultrasonic energy to cut tissue and control bleeding.
The surgeon continues to dissect, removing scar tissue that has adhered the loops of the bowel and surrounding fatty tissue to the anterior abdominal wall.
The articulated scissors are visible, maneuvering in multiple axes to precisely cut tissue during the adhesiolysis procedure.

The surgeon uses articulated scissors to dissect the small bowel from the abdominal wall, relieving the patient's partial bowel obstruction without moving the instruments in and out of the same opening.
The surgeon cauterizes a small bleeding site, indicating the nearing completion of the adhesiolysis procedure.
